text: 22.3g of fat. omega3 is where things start to fall off. for seeds, the fat profile is one of the biggest reasons they earn their place.
44.2g of carbs. starch is doing most of the heavy lifting here. for a seed, this is a very strong sign.
16.7g of protein. bioavailability is one of the best parts of the protein story. protein is a bonus, but not enough by itself.
vitamin e at 18% daily value stands out most. that is barely moving the needle.
magnesium at 92% daily value, iron at 55% daily value, calcium at 30% daily value stand out most. that gives this food real mineral support.

on the plus side: tiny food with serious nutrient density because seeds often overperform for their size. easy to sprinkle into meals because that practical versatility matters. usually brings decent fat support because that is one of the category strengths.

the drawbacks next: calorie density still stacks up fast because tiny foods can still overshoot fast. real serving sizes are often small because some strong paper numbers are hard to cash out in practice. texture or prep can be a barrier because not everyone uses seeds consistently.
B tier. Best as a support food that boosts meals instead of carrying them alone.
